title: madhuca esculenta
intro: Madhuca esculenta, also known as the Indian butter tree or mahua, is a large deciduous tree native to the forests of India and Sri Lanka, characterized by its distinctive, fragrant flowers and edible fruits. The medicinal properties of Madhuca esculenta include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of consuming Madhuca esculenta include improving digestion, reducing stress and anxiety, and boosting the immune system. In traditional medicine, Madhuca esculenta is often prepared as a herbal tea or oil, and its flowers and fruits are used to make various herbal preparations, including tonics, creams, and ointments.
